operation.title: Typed specification evaluation
operation.summary: Evaluate one typed finite measurement against explicit unit-bearing lower and/or upper limits at exact declared operating conditions.

assertion.statement: The identified finite measurement satisfies every explicit inclusive or exclusive bound at operating conditions exactly matching the specification.
non_goals: This assertion does not derive measurements, convert units, interpolate conditions, or infer omitted limits.
This assertion does not state that unlisted operating conditions, corners, samples, or specifications pass.
This assertion does not aggregate yield, optimize a design, or waive unavailable or unknown evidence.
This assertion does not upgrade a not-found or unknown measurement to engineering fail.

pass.meaning: The finite measured value and every limit use the same exact unit, every required condition exactly matches source evidence, and the value is inside every declared bound.
required_evidence: data.evaluation.status is pass and data.evaluation.measured contains one finite value and explicit unit.
data.evaluation.conditions.status is matched with equal required_count and matched_count.
Every lower or upper comparison honors its explicit inclusive flag.
data.evaluation.source retains a canonical measurement digest and its normalized-series source identity.

fail.meaning: Valid, unit-compatible, condition-matched evidence conclusively places the finite measured value outside at least one declared specification bound.
required_evidence: data.evaluation.status is fail and conditions are matched.
A specification.limit.violated diagnostic identifies the violated lower or upper bound.
The signed limiting margin is zero or negative for an exclusive boundary or below zero for an inclusive boundary violation.

unknown.meaning: The measurement is not measured, a unit or condition is incompatible or unproven, source binding is invalid, or the specification request cannot justify pass or fail.
required_evidence: At least one stable diagnostic identifies missing measurement evidence, invalid request, unit mismatch, condition mismatch, or source mismatch.
Unknown or not-found measurement input propagates to unknown and never becomes specification fail.

parameters_schema.title: specification.evaluate v1alpha1 request parameters
parameters_schema.description: One typed measurement record and one closed explicit-bound specification.

semantic_constraints: Every JSON number must be finite; NaN and infinity are invalid even if a permissive parser accepts them.
The specification and measurement identifiers must match exactly.
At least one lower or upper bound is required. If both exist, they must use the same exact unit and define a nonempty interval after inclusive flags are considered.
The measurement and every bound must use the same exact unit; v1alpha1 performs no conversion or dimensional inference.
Every required condition name must occur exactly once in measurement source evidence with the same JSON scalar type, value, and exact unit.
A measurement whose status is not_found or unknown propagates to specification unknown, never fail.
All extension objects are closed empty in v1alpha1.

facts.description: Specification pass, fail, or unknown without collapsing missing evidence into a violation.
Explicit finite measured value and unit used for a pass or fail comparison.
Explicit lower and/or upper values, units, and inclusive flags.
Exact required and matched operating-condition counts.
Signed distance to the limiting bound in the exact measurement unit.
Canonical digests and complete normalized identities for both the typed measurement and specification definition.

integrity: Require a structurally complete typed measurement with finite value for pass or fail.
Retain a canonical SHA-256 digest over the complete measurement record and its normalized-series source digest chain.
Propagate not_found and unknown measurement status to specification unknown.
artifact_roles.native_authority: The supplied typed measurement is authoritative only for the explicitly identified quantity and normalized-series source; this operation does not reinterpret native EDA evidence.

integrity: Require at least one explicit finite unit-bearing bound and exact measurement identifier.
Require unique bounded condition names with explicit scalar values and units.
Reject empty intervals and mixed bound units.
artifact_roles.native_authority: The caller-supplied closed specification definition is authoritative for this comparison only.

integrity_rules: Pass and fail both require a finite measured value, exact unit equality, exact condition binding, and valid nonempty limits.
An unavailable, not-found, malformed, unit-incompatible, or condition-incompatible measurement is unknown, not fail.
Every output retains the exact measurement digest, normalized-series source identity, complete specification definition, and specification digest used in the decision.
provenance_limitations: The operation does not independently validate the upstream measurement algorithm or optional native-artifact lineage.
Exact string equality is used for units and conditions; v1alpha1 has no unit ontology or conversion service.

features.request_effect: Evaluate one or both explicit inclusive or exclusive bounds using exact measurement units.
features.conformance_case: Fixtures cover inclusive equality, exclusive equality, inside, below, above, empty intervals, and unit mismatch.

features.request_effect: Require every named operating condition to match the typed measurement source by exact JSON scalar type, value, and unit.
features.conformance_case: Fixtures cover exact match, missing condition, value mismatch, scalar-type mismatch, unit mismatch, and duplicate names.

diagnostics.condition: The closed request, identifier, bound, interval, condition, number, or extension shape is invalid.
diagnostics.next_action: Correct the explicit typed request; do not infer missing bounds or conditions.

diagnostics.condition: The typed measurement source identity, digest chain, condition list, or lineage shape is invalid.
diagnostics.next_action: Regenerate the typed measurement from valid normalized-series evidence.

diagnostics.condition: The supplied typed measurement record is structurally inconsistent or non-finite.
diagnostics.next_action: Produce a contract-valid measurement record before specification evaluation.

diagnostics.condition: The specification measurement identifier does not match the supplied measurement.
diagnostics.next_action: Bind the exact intended measurement; do not compare a different quantity.

diagnostics.condition: The supplied measurement status is not_found or unknown and carries no finite value.
diagnostics.next_action: Produce a successful typed measurement; preserve specification status as unknown meanwhile.

diagnostics.condition: A bound or required condition unit differs from its measurement unit.
diagnostics.next_action: Supply exact matching units; v1alpha1 performs no implicit conversion.

diagnostics.condition: A required operating condition is missing or does not exactly match measurement evidence.
diagnostics.next_action: Measure at the exact required condition or revise the explicit specification binding.

diagnostics.condition: Valid unit-compatible condition-matched evidence places the measurement outside a declared bound.
diagnostics.next_action: Treat this point as engineering fail and inspect the signed margin and design behavior.

diagnostics.condition: Finite measurement and bound inputs produce a margin outside the finite JSON-number range.
diagnostics.next_action: Rescale through an explicitly declared unit conversion before evaluation; do not publish an infinite margin.

semantic_bindings.implementation: Exact-unit inclusive or exclusive lower and upper comparison with signed limiting margin, algorithm version 1.0.0.
Exact condition-name, JSON scalar-type, value, and unit subset binding, algorithm version 1.0.0.

native_actions: Validate one closed typed measurement and one closed explicit-bound specification without executing native EDA software.
Require exact measurement identifier, unit, and operating-condition bindings before comparison.
Evaluate every explicit inclusive or exclusive lower and upper bound and compute the signed limiting margin.
Preserve pass, fail, and unknown boundaries and retain the complete measurement source digest chain.
limitations: v1alpha1 supports only scalar lower and upper bounds; nominal tolerances, formulas, probabilistic limits, and cross-measurement relationships require separate versioned features.
No unit conversion, condition interpolation, waiver logic, or implicit unknown-to-fail policy is provided.

normative_surfaces: operation and assertion identifiers and pass/fail/unknown truth table
typed measurement, explicit-bound specification, and exact-condition request shapes
unit equality, interval, inclusive-boundary, condition-binding, and unknown-propagation semantics
normalized evaluation status, signed margin, measurement and specification digest chain, and diagnostic meanings
algorithm and feature identifiers and implementation version
compatibility.change_policy: Any change to bound semantics, unit or condition matching, unknown propagation, margin definition, request shape, normalized fact, or status classification requires a new operation or feature identifier. Compatible implementation corrections require a new implementation version and conformance record.
